question.text: If a trader's speculative loss from OTC derivatives is Rs. 4 lakhs and speculative income from other OTC trades is Rs. 1 lakh, what is the loss that can be carried forward?

answer.text: Nothing — speculative losses cannot be carried forward

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Speculative loss of Rs. 4 lakhs can be set off against speculative income of Rs. 1 lakh (same year). Remaining Rs. 3 lakhs can be carried forward for up to 4 assessment years, to be set off only against future speculative income.
**The Trap:** The full Rs. 4 lakhs is not carried forward — Rs. 1 lakh is already set off against speculative income. Speculative losses CAN be carried forward (but only against speculative income for 4 years).
**Cross-Exam Context:** Speculative loss set-off rules are tested in Series IV and contrast with non-speculative exchange-traded losses (8-year carry forward, broader set-off).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This restrictive treatment of OTC speculative losses is another reason to prefer exchange-traded derivatives where possible. Exchange-traded losses have more favorable tax treatment.
